title: Top 9 Yoga Wear Suppliers in China: A Bulk Buying Guide for Brands
body_html:
Finding a reliable OEM yoga apparel supplier in China is harder than it looks. Scrolling through 847 near-identical Alibaba listings at 2 a.m. is exhausting — and most of them lead nowhere.

I've sourced activewear across Guangdong and Fujian provinces for years. Here's what I know: the gap between a factory that grows your brand and one that disappears after the deposit clears comes down to about a dozen signals. Most buyers never know to check for them.

This guide gives you a verified shortlist — 9 yoga wear manufacturers I'd recommend to a friend launching a label. Each one is ranked by brand stage. You'll get real MOQs, real lead times, and the honest pros and cons that no supplier's sales rep will ever share with you.

Mingxing Clothing

MOQ: 100 pcs/color/style | Lead Time: 7–10 days sampling, 25–30 days bulk | Price Range: ~US$4.5–7.5/pc FOB

Mingxing Clothing hits a solid middle ground. The MOQs are low enough for emerging labels to get started. At the same time, the factory can handle brand-level customization without needing constant guidance. Located in Humen Town, Dongguan — one of China's busiest knitwear production zones — it runs under parent company Mingxin Clothing Co., Ltd. The factory focuses on serving global fashion and activewear brands as a private label OEM/ODM partner.

The core catalog covers the basics: sweatshirts, joggers, T-shirts, jackets, and custom sportswear basics . You won't find a dedicated yoga line on their website. But the cut-and-sew skills for high-waist leggings and sports bras are there. Check spandex content and fabric GSM during the sample stage — do this before placing a bulk order.

What Works

  • 100 pcs/color MOQ is easy to work with — great for testing colorways before you scale up

  • Production timing is clear: samples in under two weeks, bulk orders out within a month

  • The Dongguan location gives fast access to fabric sourcing and trim upgrades

What to Watch

  • No public BSCI or ISO certification listed — ask for audit reports before signing anything

  • Complex all-over prints may go to subcontractors, which can stretch lead times

  • The Alibaba profile was registered in 2024 — run a small test order first to check how consistent their operations are

Best fit for: DTC brands and cross-border sellers building a basics-anchored activewear line — logo hoodies, matching jogger sets, and private-label yoga pants in manageable batch sizes.

Berun Activewear

MOQ: Not disclosed | Lead Time: Sample-to-bulk cycle available | Price Range: Contact for quote | Experience: 15+ years in custom activewear manufacturing

Fifteen years in activewear manufacturing builds real knowledge. Berun Activewear has run OEM and ODM production long enough to watch trends rise, peak, and fade — and they're still standing.

Their core strength is a full-service production chain. They manage fabric and trim sourcing, sample development, bulk production, quality inspection, and international logistics under one roof. Burned time chasing five vendors across two provinces? This setup cuts that waste entirely. You deal with one partner, not a fragmented network.

Their catalog focuses on activewear, running apparel , cycling kits, team wear, sublimated jerseys, and event uniforms. It's performance-driven, not lifestyle-focused. That's a key difference for brands targeting athletic function over athleisure aesthetics.

What Works

  • 15+ years of OEM/ODM experience — deep production knowledge that speeds up your development cycles

  • End-to-end supply chain management cuts coordination friction across every stage

  • Serves both startup test runs and large-scale team apparel orders with equal flexibility

What to Watch

  • MOQs, pricing, and certifications aren't listed online — direct contact is the only way to get real numbers

  • The sublimation-heavy catalog means yoga-specific private label work may need detailed technical briefs from the start

Best fit for: Brands that need custom performance activewear or team/event apparel . Those who want one manufacturing partner handling everything — from first sample to final shipment — will get the most value here.

Vimost Sportswear

MOQ: Not disclosed | Lead Time: Contact for quote | Price Range: Contact for quote | Founded: 2012

Vimost Sportswear does one thing — and does it well. The factory sits in Pengzhou, Chengdu, Sichuan . Over 12 years, they've built serious depth in custom sublimated sportswear and team uniform production . Go in knowing exactly what they do before you send that first inquiry.

Their catalog centers on print-heavy performance apparel : basketball uniforms, baseball and softball kits, hockey jerseys, rugby shirts, cricket uniforms, and esports jerseys. Your brand runs on bold graphics and full-coverage sublimation? Vimost has the production capacity to back that up.

What Works

  • 12+ years of custom uniform manufacturing — real factory-floor depth that handles complex graphic specs without needing constant guidance

  • A strong match for design-driven sportswear orders where visual impact and print precision are top priorities

  • Covers a wide range of team and club apparel categories under one roof

What to Watch

  • MOQs, pricing, and compliance certifications are not confirmed — set aside extra time for your initial due diligence

  • Not a yoga or compression-wear specialist — leggings and sports bras are not part of their core production

  • Third-party revenue estimates are out there, but treat them as directory data, not audited financials

Best fit for: Custom teamwear buyers, esports apparel brands, and graphic-led sportswear projects where sublimation quality is the top priority.

Sansansun Sports

MOQ: 200–300 pcs/color/style (cut-sewn) | 300–500 pcs/color/style (seamless) | Lead Time: 7–15 days sampling, 25–40 days bulk | Price Range: ~US$4.0–6.0/pc leggings, US$5.0–8.2/set FOB

Sansansun Sports started in Dongguan in 2008. One thing sets them apart from most comparable factories: two dedicated production lines under one roof . One is a cut-and-sew facility for yoga sets, gym wear, and compression pieces. The other is a separate seamless factory for seamless leggings, bras, and crop sets.

That split matters. You no longer need to manage two vendors, two sampling cycles, and two quality inspection chains just to run a yoga line alongside a seamless collection. Sansansun covers both under one partnership.

Their catalog runs wide — high-waist yoga pants, sports bras across all support levels, biker shorts, full gym sets, running tops, and seamless collections in 1–3 piece configurations. The Pearl River Delta location keeps fabric response times short. Frequent LCL shipments stay manageable too.

What Works

  • Dual-factory setup covers cut-sewn and seamless in one deal — less fragmentation risk for your supply chain

  • Years of ODM work with European and American fitness brands means size grading, fit standards, and quality benchmarks come pre-set

  • Strong multi-SKU flexibility suits DTC brands running frequent small-batch drops

What to Watch

  • Peak season (September–November) tends to delay trims and custom elastic by 5–10 days — pre-stock LOGO labels and lock fabric capacity ahead of time

  • Certifications are not listed on their public profile. Ask for BSCI or Sedex audit reports before placing a bulk order

Best fit for: Growing DTC brands and multi-channel sellers on Amazon, TikTok Shop, or Shopee who want yoga and gym categories from a single manufacturing partner — without giving up seamless production quality.

Ninghow Apparel

MOQ: Low / Flexible (50–100 pcs for startups, 200–500 pcs standard bulk) | Lead Time: Contact for quote | Price Range: ~US$3.0–5.0/pc basics, US$4.8–7.5/pc activewear FOB | Founded: 1993

Thirty-plus years in production means something. Ninghow Apparel has supported independent designers and startup fashion brands since 1993. That's long before "private label yoga clothing" was ever typed into Google. Their factory sits in Longgang, Shenzhen. Under one roof, you get full OEM/ODM production: pattern making, cut-and-sew, embroidery, screen printing, heat transfer, and export logistics.

The "no MOQ limitations" positioning is real. They built their reputation around founders who aren't ready to commit to thousands of units per colorway. Testing fit across multiple waistband heights or inseam lengths on yoga pants bulk orders? That low-entry flexibility is exactly what makes them worth considering.

What Works

  • 10-minute email response time — faster than most factories you'll deal with in Guangdong

  • Multi-technique in-house production means logo tees, printed sports bras, and embroidered hoodies all ship from a single production chain. No juggling multiple sportswear suppliers.

  • Startup-friendly onboarding: their content walks new brands through the sampling process step by step. You won't feel lost getting started.

What to Watch

  • Activewear isn't a dedicated catalog category. For yoga pants or compression leggings, lock in spandex percentage, GSM, and squat-proof minimums in your activewear Tech Pack before sampling begins

  • Public certifications aren't confirmed. Request audit documentation before you place any order.

Best fit for: Growth-stage brands testing multiple SKUs in mid-range volumes, and first-time sourcing teams who want a manufacturing partner that actually communicates like one.

Hucai Sportswear

MOQ: 200–300 pcs/style | Lead Time: Contact for quote | Price Range: ~US$11–14.5/pc leggings (listing reference) | Founded: 1998

Twenty-five years of custom sportswear production is a real track record. Hucai started in 1998 — before most of today's activewear brands even existed. Based in Humen Town, Dongguan, they've run OEM/ODM production long enough to know exactly how Western brands buy, test, and grow.

Their catalog covers both sides of activewear. Men's options include T-shirts, shorts, vests, hoodies, sweatpants, and coats . Women's options include sports bras, high-waist leggings, and coordinated yoga sets . You get both under one roof. So building a brand with matching training basics and a yoga line means one factory, not two separate supplier deals.

What Works

  • 25 years of OEM/ODM sportswear experience — production systems and quality workflows are already in place

  • Men's training basics and women's coordinated sets from one factory — this keeps SKU development simpler and faster

  • Digital production management tools point to structured quality control, not just floor-level spot checks

What to Watch

  • Leggings start at US$11–14.5/pc — that's higher than most Dongguan factories at similar MOQs. Ask directly whether this reflects premium fabric specs or just their pricing tier

  • Certifications aren't listed on their profile — request audit documentation before you commit to an order

Best fit for: DTC activewear brands and fitness influencers building mixed-gender collections — yoga wear plus men's training basics — who want a factory with deep OEM roots and proven production processes.

Doven Clothing

MOQ: ~100–200 pcs/style/color | Lead Time: 25–35 days bulk | Price Range: ~US$3.5–5.8/pc FOB | OEM Experience: 15+ years

Doven runs through two separate entities. DOVEN CLOTHING LTD is the UK-registered legal company — you can trace it through Companies House filings. The actual production sits with Shenzhen Doven Garments Co., Ltd , based in Bao'an District, Shenzhen. They've served 200+ brands across 50+ countries since 2008, with annual revenue in the US$10–50M range. This is a mid-size OEM with solid export infrastructure — not a trading middleman pretending to be a factory.

Their catalog focuses on basics: T-shirts, polos, hoodies, jackets, and a sportswear/activewear segment covering core fitness wear. High-waist leggings and quick-dry shorts are within scope. Seamless production and structured sports bras with molded cups? That's outside their range. Knowing this upfront saves you a wasted sample cycle.

What Works

  • 100–200 pcs MOQ keeps early-stage SKU testing within budget — useful for testing 15–30 styles without heavy commitment

  • Experience across 50+ markets means packaging standards and export paperwork are routine for their team

  • Shenzhen location gives you fast fabric replenishment and easy access to consolidated shipping through a major port

What to Watch

  • No seamless production line has been confirmed — skip technical compression wear here until you verify their actual setup

  • Factory capacity details aren't listed online. Run a paid sample order first before committing to bulk

Best fit for: Early-stage DTC brands testing 15–30 SKUs at low minimums. Also works for scaling brands shifting basics production — logo tees, training shorts, everyday leggings — off their plate to protect margins on higher-complexity lines.

Modaknits Apparel

MOQ: ~200–300 pcs/color/style (estimated) | Lead Time: 3–5 days sampling, 15–30 days bulk | Price Range: ~US$2.0–3.0/pc basics, US$6.0–10.0/pc hoodies, US$5.0–8.0/pc activewear FOB | Founded: 2008

Modaknits runs five production lines out of Guangdong. They produce 180,000 units a month and add 300+ new styles to their design library every 30 days. That kind of output takes more than capacity — it takes 15+ years of being deep inside China's most active knitwear cluster, and these numbers show it.

Their certification list is built for brands selling into regulated markets: ISO, BSCI, SEDEX, Disney-audited, SGS-tested, and OEKO-TEX® Standard 100 . Your retail partners need social compliance documentation before approving a new sportswear supplier? Modaknits already has it covered. No extra paperwork chasing required.

Their core catalog covers T-shirts, hoodies, sweatpants, tracksuits, and general activewear sets. It's built for volume replenishment — not technical performance wear. That gap matters, so keep it in mind before you start sampling.

What Works

  • 300+ new designs every month — DTC brands and cross-border sellers get a steady flow of fresh inventory without building an in-house design team

  • Samples arrive in 3–5 days. That suits e-commerce operators running frequent small-batch test drops

  • The full compliance certification suite makes EU and North American retail onboarding straightforward. You won't hit documentation roadblocks

What to Watch

  • There's no dedicated high-performance yoga fabric page. Before you sample leggings, confirm nylon/spandex GSM, four-way stretch recovery, and squat-proof test results in writing

  • The 15–30 day lead time works if they're pulling from stock fabric. Custom-weight recycled polyester or specialty yarn can push delivery out to 30–45 days — lock down fabric specs and timing at the start, not at the end

Best fit for: Mid-to-large DTC and fast-fashion brands that need reliable basics replenishment — tracksuits, hoodies, everyday activewear sets — at consistent volume, backed by audit-ready compliance credentials.

Jinlong Sportswear

MOQ: 300–500 pcs/color/style (trial); 500–800 pcs standard bulk | Lead Time: Contact for quote | Price Range: ~US$2.0–3.0/pc tees, US$3.2–4.5/pc leggings FOB | Location: Huizhou, Guangdong

U.S. customs records don't lie. Jinlong — operating under Huizhou Jinlong Clothing Limited Company — shows up in import data as a repeat supplier to Reiss , the British high-street fashion brand. That's not a one-off vanity shipment. Multiple bills of lading, same client, over several years. A factory that keeps landing orders from a brand with strict supply chain standards is worth paying attention to.

The factory sits in Huizhou , close to Dongguan and Shenzhen. That puts it right inside one of the densest fabric and trim sourcing zones in southern China. For bulk order planning, that location is a real advantage.

Their sweet spot is knit basics at scale : standard-cut yoga pants in black and charcoal, quick-dry training tees, two-piece training sets. Nothing exotic. Straightforward, repeatable production.

What Works

  • Verified Western brand export history — Reiss has real compliance requirements. Factories that can't hold dimensional stability or colorfastness don't stay on their supplier list

  • Huizhou location keeps costs down. US$2.8–4.5/pc FOB is a solid target for basics-heavy bulk orders

  • Multi-line production capacity handles multi-SKU, mid-to-large runs without the scheduling headaches you'd hit at smaller factories

What to Watch

  • Not built for complexity — laser-cut panels, seamless construction, and bonded seams are outside their core capability

  • This is a larger factory. Small-batch, high-frequency development cycles will slow things down. Plan on a quarterly schedule instead

  • Public certification documents are not confirmed. Request audit reports before you send a deposit

Best fit for: Amazon and Shopee sellers scaling basics-first yoga and training lines , wholesale and off-price channel buyers, and established brands adding a cost-optimized sub-line next to their premium range.

Supplier Comparison Matrix: Capacity, Fabric Tech & Compliance

Nine suppliers. Four decision variables. One table that does the shortlisting work for you.

This comparison maps each factory across the dimensions that determine whether a sportswear supplier fits your brand stage — not their website's hero copy. Monthly capacity shows whether they can scale with you. Fabric tech tells you whether their strengths match your product. Compliance certifications decide whether your retail partners will accept them at all.

Reading the Table Without Getting It Wrong

Three things worth flagging before you start firing off inquiries.

Capacity numbers are benchmarks, not audited figures. Some factories don't publish verified production data. For those, estimates come from regional cluster norms and comparable Alibaba-tier OEMs. Use them for relative comparison. Jinlong at 800K+ pcs versus Doven at 80–120K tells you something real about production priority and scheduling flexibility. Take the exact numbers as directional guides, not hard facts.

Compliance certificates exist at two levels — factory level and fabric level. Those are different things. Several activewear suppliers here hold OEKO-TEX certification through their mill partners, not at the garment factory itself. That gap matters if your retail buyer needs factory-level audit documentation. Always ask: "Is this your factory's certificate or your fabric supplier's?"

Price ranges assume standard yoga sets at mid-spec. Recycled yarn, GRS certification, bonded seams, or proprietary wicking finishes each push costs up. Build a 10–20% buffer into any FOB estimate from this table before you run it through a landed cost model.

Conclusion

You've done the research. Now act on it.

Brands that scale and brands that stall don't split on product quality or marketing budget. The real gap is supply chain clarity. After reviewing these nine manufacturers, three things stand out:

  • MOQ flexibility matters more at launch than price

  • Seamless yoga wear production is now a baseline requirement for any serious factory

  • The right private label yoga clothing partner for a startup looks nothing like the right one for a $5M brand

Keep your shortlist tight — no more than three suppliers. Send your tech pack and a direct RFQ to each one. Do it in the same 48-hour window. Running those conversations side by side shows you fast who replies with real answers and who pastes a generic quote.

Your next move is straightforward:

  1. Pick your tier

  2. Grab two names from the comparison matrix

  3. Send that first email today

The factory that builds your brand is out there. Go reach out.

seo.title_tag: Top 9 Yoga Wear Suppliers in China: Bulk Buying Guide
seo.meta_description: Discover the top 9 yoga wear manufacturers in China with real MOQs, lead times, and honest pros & cons — the bulk buying guide every brand founder needs.

cta_blocks.body: Skip the Alibaba rabbit hole. Berun Activewear offers low MOQs, full OEM/ODM support, and transparent lead times for brands at every stage.
Our sourcing team can match you with the right factory based on your MOQ, fabric specs, and timeline. No guesswork, no wasted deposits.
From tech-pack to bulk delivery, we handle the full manufacturing process so you can focus on building your brand. Trusted by labels worldwide.
